af_score_components.notes: MCP server/proxy with flexible auth (JWT, OAuth, API key, static token) (quality 65). Multiple auth options allow adaptation to different downstream service requirements. The variety of auth methods adds agent configuration complexity — must select the right method per deployment. Rate limits depend on downstream services. Documentation is moderate. Security score (78) reflects existing evaluation. Flexible auth architecture is valuable for multi-service gateway use cases.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced for external connections. Multiple auth methods (JWT, OAuth, API key, static token) provide flexibility but increase attack surface. Moderate dependency hygiene. Credentials managed per downstream service via environment variables. Static token auth weakest option — avoid for production. JWT and OAuth preferred for stronger security. Scope granularity depends on downstream service configuration.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Moderate version stability. No formal breaking change policy documented. Reliability depends on downstream service availability. Auth method selection affects error recovery behavior. Multiple auth options require testing each path. Operational complexity scales with number of downstream services configured.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Calendar API for calendar and event management. Service account with domain-wide delegation required for accessing user calendars (Workspace). OAuth2 for single-user calendars. Recurring events require RRULE understanding. Event creation not idempotent — retrying creates duplicates. iCalendar import/export supported. Rate limits per calendar per user. Free/busy queries available without event details. Webhooks via push notifications require HTTPS endpoint.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with calendar-specific scopes (calendar.readonly, calendar.events, etc.). Service account with domain-wide delegation for Workspace automation. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance. OAuth tokens via secure flow; service account JSON via secrets manager. Domain-wide delegation grants broad access — scope appropriately. Excellent security posture as GCP service.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance with exponential backoff. Event creation not idempotent — use iCalUID for deduplication. Push notification channels expire after 1 week — must renew. Rate limits per calendar — bulk calendar operations require throttling. Calendar IDs required for all operations — must discover or store.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Classroom API for educational course management. Domain-wide delegation required for accessing student/teacher data in Workspace for Education. OAuth2 for single-user context. Requires Classroom-enabled Google Workspace for Education. Course management, assignment creation, grade submission are primary agent use cases. FERPA compliance required for student data. Rate limits per user per second. Course IDs and submission IDs required for nested operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with Classroom-specific scopes (classroom.courses, classroom.rosters, classroom.coursework.students, etc.). Domain-wide delegation for Workspace for Education. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR/FERPA compliance. OAuth tokens via secure flow. FERPA compliance critical for any student data access — explicit consent or institutional authorization required. Granular scopes enable least-privilege access per role (teacher vs student context).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Assignment submission state machine requires correct sequencing. Grade submission is not idempotent — check existing grades before submitting. Requires active Workspace for Education account — unavailable for standard Google accounts. Push notifications for submission events require HTTPS endpoint.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Docs API for document creation and editing. Service account for Workspace automation; OAuth2 for user context. Document content is structured as a tree of structural elements — complex to navigate and update programmatically. batchUpdate for atomic multi-operation edits. Document IDs from Drive required. Rate limits per project. Revision history via Drive API. No partial document reads — always fetches full document. Better for structured edits than freeform text manipulation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with Docs-specific scopes (documents, documents.readonly). Service account with domain-wide delegation for Workspace. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance. OAuth tokens via secure flow. Document access governed by Drive sharing permissions + API scopes. Service account must be shared on documents it needs to access. Excellent GCP security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. batchUpdate operations are atomic — partial failures roll back. Full document fetch can be expensive for large documents — no range queries. Revision conflicts require etag handling for concurrent edits. Rate limits per project — batch operations reduce quota consumption.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Drive API for file storage and management. Service account with domain-wide delegation for Workspace; OAuth2 for user context. Files in Drive have MIME types — Google Workspace files (Docs, Sheets) require export for download. Resumable uploads for large files. Drive shared drives require different API paths than My Drive. Rate limits per user per second. Push notifications for file changes. File IDs are stable; paths are not — always use IDs.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with fine-grained Drive scopes (drive, drive.readonly, drive.file, drive.appdata, drive.metadata.readonly). Service account with domain-wide delegation for Workspace automation. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR/HIPAA compliance. OAuth tokens via secure flow. drive.file scope limits access to files created by the app — excellent least-privilege option. Shared drive permissions separate from My Drive.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with SLA. Stable versioned API. Excellent migration guides. Retry guidance with exponential backoff. Resumable uploads handle large file reliability. File operations idempotent with correct handling of 410/404 on resumable uploads. Push notification channels expire — must renew. Shared drive operations have different quota buckets from My Drive.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Maps Platform API suite — Places, Geocoding, Directions, Distance Matrix, Routes. Simple API key. Application and API restrictions on API keys enable least-privilege. Rate limits clearly documented per API. Places API (new) vs Places API (legacy) — use New for new integrations. Geocoding is idempotent. Distance Matrix is powerful for logistics agents. Maps JavaScript API is client-side only — use server-side APIs for agent workflows. Billing per request type — monitor costs.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key with application restrictions (HTTP referrer, IP) and API restrictions (specific Maps APIs). Restriction by IP best for server-side agent use. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance. API key via environment variable. Keys can be scoped to specific APIs — excellent least-privilege for Maps use cases. Never use unrestricted API keys in production. Billing alerts recommended.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with excellent SLA and global status. Stable versioned APIs. Excellent migration guides (Places API legacy to new). Retry guidance with exponential backoff. Full idempotency on geocoding and place lookups. Distance Matrix results are deterministic for same inputs. Billing quotas require monitoring — unexpected spikes can exhaust budget. Routes API preferred over legacy Directions for new integrations.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for Google Search access (quality 50). No auth required — scraping-based or Custom Search JSON API. Rate limits from Google's anti-scraping measures are opaque and unpredictable. Scraping-based approaches may violate Google ToS. Custom Search JSON API (official) requires API key and Custom Search Engine ID — limited to configured sources. Results quality varies from official Google search. Better alternatives exist (Exa, SerpAPI) for production search agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS to Google search. No credentials required (if scraping-based). Unknown dependency hygiene for scraping libraries. Scraping approach risks Google ToS violations. Custom Search JSON API (official alternative) uses API key with search-only scope — more secure. No credentials stored for scraping variant. Terms of service compliance is the primary concern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted, dependent on Google search availability and anti-bot measures. Unknown version — scraping layer may break without notice. No documented change policy. GET queries idempotent. Rate limiting from Google's side causes unpredictable failures. Not suitable for production workflows requiring consistent, reliable search. Custom Search JSON API alternative more reliable but limited to configured search engine.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Google Cloud Translation API — v3 (advanced) preferred over v2 (basic). Multiple auth: API key (simple), OAuth2, or service account. Language auto-detection available. Batch translation via BatchTranslateText for large volumes. Glossaries for custom terminology. Model selection: general vs nmt variants. Rate limits documented per character. Supports 100+ languages. Full idempotency on translation calls. v3 adds document translation and glossary support over v2.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Multiple auth: API key (simple), service account (recommended for production), OAuth2. Cloud Translation API scope. Google-maintained. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR compliance. API key or service account JSON via environment variable. API key restriction to Cloud Translation API available. Translation content is sent to Google — review data retention for sensitive workflows. Batch translation for efficiency.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Google Cloud infrastructure with SLA. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides (v2 to v3). Built-in retry with exponential backoff. Full idempotency on translation (same text + language pair = same result). Character quota monitoring required for budget control. Batch translation operations are async for large datasets. Language detection included in translation calls.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for Google Workspace (quality 68). Covers Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs integration via OAuth. Single MCP server replacing multiple individual Google API integrations. OAuth flow complexity — must complete browser-based auth before MCP tools work. Rate limits from underlying Google APIs per service. Error messages from Google APIs pass through MCP layer. Useful for unified Workspace access but OAuth setup is non-trivial for agents. Domain-wide delegation not supported in all implementations.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS via Google APIs. OAuth2 with Workspace scopes. OAuth client ID/secret required — must create GCP project. Third-party MCP server — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer. OAuth tokens stored locally for session persistence. Scope selection critical — request minimal required scopes. Client credentials (client_id, client_secret) must be protected. Not official Google product — trust accordingly.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on Google API uptime (excellent) and MCP server stability (moderate). MCP server versioning independent of Google API versions. OAuth token refresh must work reliably for long-running agents. Breaking changes in underlying Google APIs may require MCP server updates. Multiple Google API rate limits (Calendar, Gmail, Drive) apply simultaneously. Error propagation through MCP layer adds debugging complexity.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. AI-powered research assistant that orchestrates LLM + search APIs for automated research reports. Requires API keys for multiple services (OpenAI/Anthropic + search providers). Report generation takes minutes — agents must handle long-running operations. Multiple API integration points create high failure probability. Self-hosted with no managed service option. Quality depends heavily on configured LLM and search provider quality. Not a simple API — full application stack.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS to LLM and search providers. Multiple API keys required for LLM + search services — broad secret management surface. No scope granularity. Unknown dependency hygiene for open source tool. Multiple API keys in environment variables creates credential sprawl. Self-hosted means operator is responsible for security hardening. Research results and queries sent to multiple external services.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — actively developed. No documented breaking change policy. Multiple external API dependencies (LLM + search) compound failure probability. Report generation not idempotent — same query may produce different results. 2-5 minute report generation creates timeout challenges. Rate limits from each underlying provider apply. Not suitable for production agent workflows requiring consistent, fast results.
